$comment: vibe-mybooks-compatibility-addendum §3.11 — appliance manifest. Keep in sync with docker-compose.appliance.yml. The appliance reads this file to know how to compose Vibe MyBooks alongside its sibling apps. Schema authority: console/manifest.schema.json in the (forthcoming) Vibe-Appliance repo.
description: Self-hosted bookkeeping with magic-link client portal

image.$comment: defaultTag is `latest` for ergonomics — the appliance admin UI offers a tag picker. Production deployments should pin to a specific minor (e.g. 1.4) so a rolling :latest doesn't surface a breaking schema change mid-deploy.

env.$comment: ALLOWED_ORIGIN in the addendum's template maps to CORS_ORIGIN in this repo (the canonical name in packages/api/src/config/env.ts). The manifest reflects reality.

optional.name: MIGRATIONS_AUTO
optional.doc: Appliance runs migrations as a one-shot before api starts; api refuses to start with pending migrations.

optional.name: COOKIE_SECURE
optional.doc: Always-HTTPS in multi-app mode. Force off (=false/0) for the emergency-access proxy at port 5171.

optional.name: COOKIE_PATH
optional.doc: Set to /mybooks for multi-app mode so cookies don't leak into sibling apps on the same origin.

optional.name: EXPECT_WORKER
optional.doc: When true, /api/v1/health workers sub-check fails if no worker heartbeat in last 30s. Disable for api-only deployments.

optional.name: TRUST_PROXY
optional.doc: Express trust-proxy setting. Required behind Caddy so req.ip / req.protocol see the real client and don't break IP allowlists.

optional.name: SMTP_HOST
optional.doc: SMTP server for password resets, magic links, portal invitations, and reminder emails. Optional in env.ts but practically required in the appliance.

optional.name: DISABLE_LICENSE_CHECK
optional.doc: Appliance ships with license check disabled until the operator pastes a key via the appliance admin console (vibe-mybooks-compatibility-addendum §3.8 footnote).

optional.name: LICENSE_TOKEN
optional.doc: RS256 JWT issued by licensing.kisaes.com. Required when DISABLE_LICENSE_CHECK=0.

optional.name: LICENSE_PUBLIC_KEY
optional.doc: PEM-format RSA public key matching the issuer that signed LICENSE_TOKEN.

optional.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
optional.doc: Claude API key for AI receipt OCR / categorization features.

optional.name: WEBAUTHN_RP_ID
optional.doc: Passkey RP identifier — defaults to URL(PUBLIC_URL).hostname. Set explicitly when sibling apps share the same hostname and you want passkey isolation.

optional.name: JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRY
optional.doc: Short-lived staff access-token TTL. Tighter values (e.g. 5m) increase forced-refresh frequency but reduce the impact of a stolen token.

optional.name: JWT_ADMIN_MAX_AGE
optional.doc: Admin-route session-age cap; super-admin who walks away from the appliance must re-login before the admin panel accepts the next click.

database.$comment: The appliance provisions this Postgres role/database (distinct from the standalone `kisbooks` default — single-tenant appliances may share a Postgres cluster across apps).
database.name: vibe_mybooks_db

firstLogin.note: First registered user becomes the super-admin (and acts as the primary firm admin on a single-firm appliance). Send the registration URL to the operator who will own the appliance.

backup.$comment: volumes = named Docker volumes for file-based backup; databases = Postgres database names for pg_dump. The appliance backs up both. `vibe-mybooks-backups` was deliberately removed from the volumes list — running an in-app backup writer alongside the appliance's own backup tooling produces double-encrypted redundant snapshots.
